summary: Patient Data Encryption and Audit Trails Test Case
description: Verify that patient data is encrypted using AES-256 and audit trails are stored for every database change.
objective: Validate compliance with FDA Part 11 and ISO 9001 regulations regarding data encryption and integrity.
prerequisites: System must be configured to use AES-256 encryption
Database must be set up to store audit trails

test_steps.description: Enter a new patient record into the system
Inspect the stored patient data to verify it is encrypted with AES-256
Trigger a database change (e.g. update, delete) to test audit trail functionality
Observe the system's response to verify that an audit trail is created for the database change

expected_result: Data is encrypted with AES-256 and an audit trail is stored for every database change.
test_data: Sample patient data (e.g. name, date of birth, medical history)
acceptance_criteria: Audit trails are stored correctly for every database change
